description_long: This object is a Roland SH-101 synthesizer keyboard, featuring a rectangular body with a matte reddish-pink finish. The front panel is densely populated with various knobs, sliders, and switches, each labeled with white text for different sound modulation and control functions. The keyboard section consists of black and white keys, typical of a musical keyboard, and is situated along the lower half of the front face. The top left area contains modulation wheels and additional controls, while the right side displays the Roland logo and model name prominently. The rear and side panels are plain, with no visible ports or connectors in these views. The overall design is compact and portable, suitable for use in music production or live performance settings.
description: A compact Roland SH-101 synthesizer keyboard with a reddish-pink body, white and black keys, and numerous labeled control knobs and sliders on the front panel.
description_view_0: The front view shows the full keyboard, all control knobs, sliders, switches, and the Roland SH-101 branding.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the thin profile of the keyboard, with some knobs and controls slightly protruding.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a plain, unadorned reddish-pink surface with no visible features or connectors.
description_view_3: The right side view mirrors the left, showing the slim profile and some protruding controls.
attribute_key_layout: Synthesizer piano-style layout (32 keys, no numpad)
